parsed_review.summary: CP00-503 is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only governance-core closeout pack (RP16.P06.M06.S05-RP16.P07.M02.S06, 150 units, risk C). The pack-level readiness claim is fully supported and not overstated: manifest.json and command-evidence.json both declare production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:"descriptor", no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false. The implementation is genuinely descriptor-only — service.js (2961-3036) emits every row with descriptor_only:true / runtime_execution:false / raw_payload_included:false, and freezeCp503Result (2917) stamps a comprehensive no-runtime/no-write/no-DB/no-object-storage/no-Hermes-runtime-receipt/no-leak attestation. Unit distribution (150) and deliverable/micro-phase/micro-title counts agree across registry requirements, the contract projection (current_pack + p06_closeout_p07_foundation_descriptor), the closeout-pack-plan (pack present at line 408 with matching ledger SHA), and the review baseline; validateGovernanceCoreCp503Coverage and the dedicated contract validator (scripts/validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs) enforce drift via deepEqual and per-section checks. Authority boundary holds: Claude is bound to read-only Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval:false, no enterprise-trust claim from local validation — enforced in validators.js (4301-4308) and exercised in model.test.js (1604, 1621-1622). Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt shapes, not product runtime. Review-hardening posture is clean: readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1226-byte valid JSON, is_error:false), no invalid prior attempts, and no malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence to reject. README and validation evidence are consistent; unrelated dirty files are correctly segregated. The three post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are appropriately absent because they are products of this review's downstream hardening sequence (status implemented_pending_review). Only two non-blocking P3 observations are recorded; no P0/P1/P2 issues. Human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim, which is outside this receipt.

findings.title: Post-review evidence artifacts declared in manifest are not yet present (expected for pending-review state)
findings.details: manifest.evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list five files, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist; claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are absent. This is correct and expected: the pack status is implemented_pending_review and pack_level_claude_review.status is pending, and those three artifacts are produced by the downstream hardening sequence (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection) that consumes THIS review's output. Their absence is not a defect at review time.
findings.recommendation: No action for this review. Ensure the post-review closeout gate (npm run closeout-pack:validate / receipt:validate) enforces presence of all five required artifacts before commit, so the pre-declared evidence_refs cannot be closed out without them.

findings.title: RP16.P07 sections carry foundational micro_titles while their rows are failure-recovery taxonomy rows
findings.details: required_section_micro_titles labels RP16.P07.M00 'Scope Inventory', M01 'Contract Draft', and M02 'Type And Shape Definition', yet required_section_rows for those micro-phases contain failure-recovery row titles (Failure taxonomy, Missing tenant failure, Missing actor failure, etc.). This is a labeling artifact inherited verbatim from the weighted implementation ledger (source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65...) and is identical across the registry, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, contract projection, baseline, and closeout-pack-plan.json (deviation_from_plan:false). It is internally consistent and not a pack-level deviation, but the micro_title/row-content semantics are mismatched at the generator level.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-503; the pack faithfully reflects its authoritative plan. If clearer semantics are desired, address the micro_title assignment in the upstream weighted-ledger generator rather than in any individual pack.
